titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Meryle Secrest Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff and Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 3 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 1,437 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2025 at 00:06

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Meryle Secrest Papers, which span from 1900 to 2019, contain writings, notes, correspondence, research materials, and computer and audiovisual media related to the life, work, and primarily the writings, of Meryle Secrest. Correspondents include Svetlana Allilueva, Kenneth Clark, Hugh Leonard, H. R. Trever-Roper, and Herman Wouk. The collection also contains photographs of Amedeo Modigliani and Elsa Schiaparelli.

bioghist.head: Meryle Secrest
bioghist.p: Meryle Secrest is a journalist and biographer. Born in Bath, England, she emigrated to Canada with her family, and eventually to the United States, where she worked for the Washington Post from 1964 to 1975. Secrest left the Washington Post to focus on writing books, and has published a number of biographies, including Being Bernard Berenson (Harmondsworth, 1980), which was a finalist for the 1980 Pulitzer Prize in Biography. In 2006 she received the National Humanities Medal.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Writings and Research, 1970-2011. II. Correspondence, 1900-2009. III. Photographs and Research Materials, 1900-2011. IV. Index Cards and Subject Files, 1970-2005. V. Interviews, 1990-2008. VI. Computer Media, circa 2000-2009. VII. November 2019 Acquisition, 1930-2011. VIII. January 2022 Acquisition, 1975-2019.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 21-24 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 25, 31 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Meryle Secrest and Lion Heart Autographs, Inc. on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2015.
November 2019 acquisition: Purchased from June Meryle Beveridge and Lion Heart Autographs, Inc. on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2019
January 2022 acquisition: Gift of Meryle Secrest, 2022.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Meryle Secrest Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Meryle Secrest Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Index cards created by Secrest during her biographical research projects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include chapter notes and changes.

did.unittitle: Mary Rodgers Guettel and Linda Rodgers Emory interview
Linda Rodgers Emory interview and related materials
Arthur Laurents interview and related materials

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 21-24 (Audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains audio recordings of interviews performed during Secrest's biographical research projects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence includes copies of Frank Lloyd Wright's business and family correspondence as well as correspondence generated by Secrest while doing research for her book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include correspondence between Secrest and Sondhiem and other correspondence pertaining to Secrest's biograpy of Sondheim.

did.unittitle: Barry Drogin correspondence relating to Sondheim
Keith Baxter correspondence relating to Sondheim

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence Secrest received while doing research for her book on Clark.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence Secrest received while doing research for her book on Modigliani.

did.unittitle: Senda Berenson Abbott's correspondence with her family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence Secrest received while doing research for her book on Duveen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence Secrest recieved while doing research for her book on Brooks.

did.unittitle: Materials related to the Romaine Brooks estate
Ian Curteis correspondence relating to Bernard Berenson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence Secrest received while doing research for her biography on Rodgers.

did.unittitle: Berenson family correspondence and other materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include autographs, along with personal correspondence and correspondence from and to others. Autographs with correspondence included are Jean Hugo, Barbara Cook, David Cornwell (John le Carré), Benjamin Spock M.D., Roddy McDowall, William Coldstream, Katerina Wilczynski, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence Secrest received while doing research for her biography on Berenson.

scopecontent.p: Correspondence Secrest received while doing research for her biography on Berenson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence Secrest received while doing research for her biography on Berenson.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

Romaine Brooks research materials and drawing
Notes and transcript titled "Audition Tapes, SS songs"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials include notes and research materials most likely related to Secrest's Stephen Sondheim biography.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of and relating to Amedeo Clemente Modigliani.

Photographs of Bernard Berenson and related materials
Photographs and negatives of or related to Romaine Brooks

Photographs relating to and of Joseph Duveen

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts, notes, clippings, primarily related to research interviews performed by Secrest.

did.physdesc: 23.62 Megabytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence related to Secrest's life and work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs related to Secrest's biographical research projects.
